text:
No, muscle does not turn into fat if you stop exercising. Muscle and fat are two different types of tissue and cannot transform into each other.

What actually happens is:  
- If you stop exercising, your muscles may shrink (atrophy) from lack of use.
- If you also eat more calories than you burn, you may gain fat.
- This can make it look or feel like muscle has "turned into" fat, but in reality, you are losing muscle mass and gaining fat.

Maintaining an active lifestyle and eating a balanced diet can help preserve muscle and prevent fat gain.
